notes: Weekly Rhythm skill now deploys the full engine specification — first-run setup, MCP health checks, and the complete Step 1-12 pipeline all work correctly
Previously only a thin wrapper was deployed, which caused Claude to improvise instead of following the actual engine instructions
Weekly Rhythm skill now deploys to Claude's user skills folder — say "run my weekly rhythm" in any Claude session to use it
Fort Abode keeps the skill wrapper up to date automatically on every launch
Weekly Rhythm Engine now installs as a proper Claude Code plugin — it shows up natively in Claude's skills list and sticks around between restarts
Tap 'Install Plugin in Claude Code' on the Weekly Rhythm screen to install or reinstall. The raw CLI output is shown inline so you can see exactly what happened
Feedback reports moved to the shared 'Kennedy Family Docs/Claude/Fort Abode Feedback' folder so reports from both Kamren's and Tiera's Macs show up on both machines via iCloud
Existing feedback files from the old per-machine folder get migrated automatically on first launch
Under the hood: stopped writing directly to Claude Code's internal skill files — everything now goes through the official plugin CLI so updates won't get clobbered
Feedback reports now save directly to your shared Fort Abode Logs folder in iCloud — no setup, no tokens, no character limits
Fixed a bug where feedback submissions failed silently when the debug report was long
After submitting, Fort Abode shows you the exact file path so you can grab the report or share it yourself
New "Register in Claude" button on the Weekly Rhythm screen — tap it if the skill ever stops appearing in your Claude skills list and see the result inline
Fort Abode now logs every step of skill registration so bug reports show exactly what happened — no more guessing
More reliable error logging — messages save to both iCloud and your Mac, so nothing gets dropped when iCloud is slow or offline
Fort Abode logs now live in their own "Fort Abode Logs" folder inside Claude Memory instead of loose at the top level

Fixed Weekly Rhythm skill silently failing to register on machines with partial iCloud state — the self-heal now runs whenever the app detects an existing install, instead of requiring every managed file to be present
Partial installs self-repair on launch: missing engine-spec.md or dashboard-template.html get redeployed from the app bundle before the skill registers with Claude Code
Weekly Rhythm skill now re-registers with Claude Code on every app launch — fixes the skill not appearing on some family machines after install
New components no longer auto-deploy on update — they stay in the marketplace until you explicitly install them
Clearer error logging when Claude Code isn't ready for skill registration — bug reports now capture exactly what went wrong
Fixed a display bug where the Travel Itinerary component showed "vconfigured" instead of "Configured"
Memory component now offers family memory during setup — one person enables it, the other sees it automatically
Fixed a long-standing bug where iCloud folders weren't actually being kept downloaded on some machines
Claude Memory and Weekly Flow folders now re-pin every time the app opens
Weekly Rhythm skill now auto-registers in Claude Code on install
Existing installs self-heal — stale skill files get replaced automatically
Uninstalling Weekly Rhythm cleanly removes the Claude Code skill entry
Send feedback, bug reports, and feature ideas directly from the app
What's New now shows all updates you missed — not just the latest
Toolbar separator fix for all family machines
Translucent glass look for the What's New screen
Weekly Rhythm Engine now available in the Marketplace
Install it to get your weekly planning dashboard set up automatically
Engine files stay up to date with every app update — no manual copying
Memory servers now use your name — "Tiera-Memory" instead of generic labels
Setup wizard asks your name when installing Memory for the first time
Existing installs get a one-time prompt to personalize
App updates now download silently and install on next relaunch
Bugs and stability improvements
